title: oligoneuron houghtonii
intro: Oligoneuron houghtonii, also known as the Houghton's goldenrod, is a perennial plant species in the aster family that is native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Oligoneuron houghtonii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. Oligoneuron houghtonii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for its medicinal uses, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in herbal remedies.
